title: Fix Windows compilation: add signgam compat header for core-math lgamma.c

comments.author: phase-4-coordinator
comments.content: Plan: Add a build.rs to ferray-ufunc that detects Windows target and injects a compat header declaring signgam via CFLAGS/DEP_CORE_MATH_* env. Also add the compat header file itself. This works around core-math-sys vendored lgamma.c using POSIX-only signgam global. Ref: GitHub issue dollspace-gay/ferray#5

comments.author: phase-4-coordinator
comments.content: core-math-sys already ships lib/signgam.c with 'int signgam;' and compiles it into the static lib. The issue is that lgamma.c does '#include <math.h> // for signgam' which declares signgam on POSIX but not on Windows. The fix is to declare signgam as extern in a compat header and inject it via CFLAGS. But since core-math-sys is an upstream dep with its own build.rs, we can't modify its compilation. Instead, we need to add the compat header at the ferray-ufunc level using a build.rs that sets the CFLAGS env var before core-math-sys compiles.

comments.author: phase-4-coordinator
comments.content: Actually, CFLAGS set in a downstream build.rs won't affect an upstream -sys crate's cc::Build. The cc crate reads CFLAGS from the environment at cargo invocation time, not from DEP_ vars. The correct approach: set CFLAGS='-include signgam_compat.h' as a cargo config env var for Windows targets. We'll create a .cargo/config.toml entry and document the workaround, plus create the compat header.
